rationale: Sales/marketing professional with only 1.6 years of experience, no engineering or ML background, and beginner-level skills in ETL/GCP/Webpack with no relevance to IR or ranking systems.

Despite a Stanford PhD and 'Embeddings advanced' skill claim, the actual career descriptions show Android development, Java backend, and DevOps work, with two fictional companies (Wayne Enterprises, Dunder Mifflin) flagging this as a honeypot profile with no real IR or retrieval experience.

9.2 years entirely in non-technical operations management, project management, and business analyst consulting roles with no ML or engineering work, and a stint at fictional Stark Industries reinforcing this as an anti-persona candidate.

Solid data engineering background with XGBoost and sklearn experience at Freshworks (legit SaaS product company), but top skills are Computer Vision, Time Series, and ASR with no embedding-based retrieval, vector search, or NDCG/ranking evaluation experience.

13+ years entirely in non-technical roles (Sales, HR Manager, Business Analyst) at IT-services firms (Wipro, Infosys) and fictional company Dunder Mifflin, with zero evidence of engineering or ML work.

4.9 years of applied ML engineering at product companies InMobi (AdTech) and Mad Street Den (AI/ML startup), with production recommendation systems using collaborative filtering, XGBoost, NLP classification pipelines, pgvector advanced, and NIT Trichy tier_1 education — good applied ML foundation but lacks explicit BM25/hybrid retrieval and ranking evaluation metric (NDCG/MAP) experience.

8 years of experience entirely in QA engineering, frontend development, and DevOps at IT-services firms Capgemini and Infosys plus fictional Initech, with SEO as a top skill and no ML or information retrieval work.

14.8 years in completely non-technical roles (Civil Engineer title, HR Manager, Sales Executive, Content Writer) at fictional companies Pied Piper and Wayne Enterprises, with no engineering or ML background whatsoever.

Entire career is marketing/content writing at Wipro (IT services) with mechanical engineer titles mismatched to marketing descriptions; 'Information Retrieval advanced' skill has only 4 endorsements and 16 months duration, indicating keyword inflation with no actual IR engineering experience.

5.1 years in operations management, accounting, and customer support at fictional Dunder Mifflin and IT-services TCS, with no technical engineering or ML skills relevant to ranking or retrieval systems.
